LootWeaponArmorTypes.brief: A collection of constants representing armor parts and weapon types used for item categorization in the loot system.
LootWeaponArmorTypes.details: The LootWeaponArmorTypes group defines a set of constant integer identifiers for various armor components and weapon types utilized within the game's loot and equipment management systems. Armor constants cover multiple body parts such as head, torso, legs, arms, hands, wrists, waist, and specialized categories like floating armor and shields, enabling precise classification and handling of armor items. Weapon constants include a variety of weapon types like axes, daggers, swords, bows, and exotic categories, supporting diverse combat and equipment options. These standardized identifiers promote consistent item categorization, simplify loot processing, and facilitate maintainable code across different game systems.
LootWeaponArmorTypes.notes: The constants are organized into armor and weapon categories, with each type assigned a unique identifier to ensure clarity and ease of maintenance. This organization supports modular handling of equipment and loot items, allowing for straightforward expansion or modification of armor and weapon types.
LootWeaponArmorTypes.rationale: This group exists to provide a clear, maintainable, and standardized set of identifiers for armor and weapon types, which are essential for consistent item categorization, processing, and gameplay mechanics. By centralizing these constants, the system ensures uniform handling across various modules, reducing errors and simplifying future updates or extensions to the loot and equipment systems.
LootWeaponArmorTypes.name: LootWeaponArmorTypes

ARMOR_LIGHT.brief: Identifier for light armor types within the loot and equipment system.
ARMOR_LIGHT.details: This constant assigns the unique integer value 100 to represent light armor categories in the game's loot management system. It is used to classify and differentiate light armor items from other armor types, such as medium or heavy armor, throughout the codebase. The constant is defined within the LootWeaponArmorTypes namespace, ensuring consistent referencing across various modules handling item categorization, loot generation, and equipment logic.
ARMOR_LIGHT.notes: The constant is stored as a constexpr to enable compile-time optimization and ensure immutability. Its value is used in conditional checks and item classification routines to facilitate efficient armor type handling.
ARMOR_LIGHT.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable identifier for light armor types, supporting consistent categorization and processing of armor items within the game's loot and equipment systems.
ARMOR_LIGHT.name: ARMOR_LIGHT

ARMOR_FINGER.brief: Constant representing the armor type for finger armor in the loot system.
ARMOR_FINGER.details: The `ARMOR_FINGER` constant is used to identify and categorize finger armor items within the game's loot and equipment management system. Initialized with the value 101, it serves as a unique identifier for all finger armor pieces, enabling consistent referencing across various modules such as loot generation, item filtering, and equipment handling. Its use ensures that finger armor can be easily distinguished from other armor types, supporting clear and maintainable code logic related to armor categorization.
ARMOR_FINGER.notes: Typically stored as an integer code within item data structures, facilitating efficient comparisons and lookups during gameplay operations.
ARMOR_FINGER.rationale: This constant exists to provide a standardized identifier for finger armor, simplifying item classification and ensuring consistent behavior when processing or generating loot involving this specific armor type.
ARMOR_FINGER.name: ARMOR_FINGER

ARMOR_NECK.brief: Constant representing the unique identifier for neck armor type in the loot categorization system.

It is used to categorize, filter, and process neck armor separately from other armor types during loot generation, item sorting, and equipment handling. Located within the LootWeaponArmorTypes namespace, it ensures consistent referencing across various modules that deal with item classification, loot tables, and inventory management. Its usage facilitates clear and maintainable code when distinguishing neck armor from other equipment categories.
ARMOR_NECK.notes: The constant is stored as a constexpr integer, enabling compile-time optimization and ensuring its value remains immutable throughout the program execution.
ARMOR_NECK.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable, and efficient way to identify neck armor items within the game's categorization system, supporting consistent item handling and loot processing logic.
ARMOR_NECK.name: ARMOR_NECK

ARMOR_TORSO.brief: Constant representing the armor type identifier for torso protection in the loot system.

ARMOR_TORSO.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify torso armor items, facilitating consistent categorization and processing within the loot system, and supporting game mechanics that depend on armor type distinctions.
ARMOR_TORSO.name: ARMOR_TORSO

WIELD_AXE.details: This constant defines the unique integer identifier used to specify that a character or entity is wielding an axe as their weapon within the loot and equipment management system. Initialized with the value 200, it is used throughout the codebase to categorize, process, and differentiate axe-wielding items and actions. As part of the LootWeaponArmorTypes set, it ensures consistent referencing and handling of axe weapon types across various modules and functions.

WIELD_AXE.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, standardized identifier for axe weapons, facilitating consistent handling, categorization, and processing of such items within the game's loot and equipment systems.
WIELD_AXE.name: WIELD_AXE

WIELD_DAGGER.notes: The constant is stored as a constexpr, ensuring compile-time evaluation and optimization. Its value is fixed and should not be modified during runtime.
WIELD_DAGGER.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, consistent identifier for dagger weapons, enabling the game to distinguish dagger-specific behavior and interactions from other weapon types efficiently.
WIELD_DAGGER.name: WIELD_DAGGER

It facilitates checks and logic related to whether a character can wield or equip exotic weapons or armor, often in conjunction with other item type identifiers. WIELD_EXOTIC.notes: The constant is stored as an integer and is part of a set of item type identifiers, enabling efficient comparisons and categorization within the game's item management system.
WIELD_EXOTIC.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference the 'exotic' item category, supporting game mechanics that restrict or allow exotic gear based on character abilities or class restrictions.
WIELD_EXOTIC.name: WIELD_EXOTIC
